name: Herb Hall
abstract: Herbert L. Hall (March 28, 1907 – March 5, 1996) was an American jazz clarinetist and alto saxophonist.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
Jazz Concert in Memory of Sidney Bechet
Bob Greene's "World of Jelly Roll Morton"

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Polcer
about_quotes.evidence: Herb Hall was the original one, Edmond Hall’s brother, he was there for three years with us. He was our original clarinetist
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Polcer interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Herb Hall was on clarinet. How long were you with Sam Price? ... [INTERVIEWEE] Just that tour, yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Herb Hall. >> [INTERVIEWEE] Herb Hall on clarinet.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: And then there's Herb Hall on clarinet, Edmond Hall's brother.

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: he hired people like I can think of first Edmund Hall and later his brother Herb Hall at his club
